How certain is the authorship of Marcus Aurelius's Meditations (沉思錄)?

The authorship of the Meditations (沉思錄) is classified as 'attributed,' meaning there is strong scholarly consensus that Marcus Aurelius, the Roman Emperor who lived from 121 to 180 CE, wrote the text. The Meditations appear to be a private journal of Stoic self-reflection, never intended for publication, which lends some authenticity to its personal voice. However, since no autograph manuscript survives and the text was transmitted through later copies, absolute certainty in the modern scholarly sense cannot be claimed. It is important not to attribute this work to other Stoic figures such as Epictetus, Plato, or Socrates — Marcus Aurelius was a distinct author working centuries after those thinkers.
